nativeRange.habitat: Outer reef slopes and clear lagoon reefs, typically in areas of mixed living and dead coral with numerous shelter holes and passages; recorded from 1–56 m depth.

diet.notes: Best fed a varied marine diet that includes spirulina, marine algae, high-quality angelfish preparations, and meaty foods such as enriched frozen brine and mysis shrimp. In a mature aquarium with live rock they will graze opportunistically throughout the day; feeding 2–3 times daily is generally advised.

summary: A rarely-collected dwarf angelfish from the Mariana and Ogasawara Islands, with bright orange-yellow body coloration and electric-blue vertical barring on the upper half. Generally considered reef-safe with caution.
careNotes: The True Shepard's Angelfish is a remote-Western-Pacific dwarf angel, sometimes traded as the Mango Angelfish. It is generally advised to introduce this species after more passive fish are established to reduce territorial pressure, and to provide plenty of live rock with shelter holes and open swimming space. Reef compatibility is generally good when the fish is well fed; isolated cases of nipping at LPS coral polyps and clam mantles have been reported. Sources disagree somewhat on minimum tank size: LiveAquaria recommends 70 US gallons (~265 L), while other care guides cite anywhere from 55 gallons (~210 L) to 500 L; the 300 L figure used here biases toward the larger side because dwarf angelfish are territorial and benefit from open swimming space. Adult size also differs across sources — LiveAquaria states up to 3.5–4 inches (~9–10 cm) while FishBase records a maximum of 9 cm TL; the 9–11 cm range used here brackets both. Like other Centropyge, this species is protogynous (female-first) and naturally forms small harems with one dominant male; aquarists generally keep them singly or as a male-female pair rather than multiple conspecifics. Like most dwarf angels, it can be sensitive to copper-based medications at higher therapeutic doses.
breedingNotes: Captive breeding is rarely achieved and is considered advanced. Like other Centropyge species, the True Shepard's Angelfish is a protogynous hermaphrodite that forms harems in the wild, with the dominant individual transitioning to male. Pelagic spawners; larval rearing is difficult and not currently established at hobbyist scale.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Pomacanthidae, Acanthuriformes), adult size (FishBase records 9.0 cm TL max vs LiveAquaria 3.5–4 in / 9–10 cm — minor disagreement, used a conservative 9–11 cm range), depth range (1–56 m), native range (Mariana and Ogasawara Islands; possible population SW of Palau), and habitat. IUCN status Least Concern.

additional.name: AquariumCodex / aquarium hobby care guides
additional.notes: Tertiary cross-check for tank size. Hobby guides span 55 gal (~210 L) to 500 L recommendations; 70 gal/~265 L is the most commonly cited minimum. Rounded up to 300 L here to account for dwarf-angel territoriality and the need for open swimming space.

fish.breedingNotes: Protogynous hermaphrodite; haremic spawner. Captive breeding very rarely achieved.
